extra.reasoning: The invariant (opted_out implies not sent) is an inductive invariant of this state machine. sent becomes true ONLY via t_send_allowed, which is guarded by `not opted_out`, so at the moment sent is set true, opted_out is false. opted_out becomes true ONLY via t_optout, whose update atomically sets sent := false in the same step, so the post-state of t_optout has opted_out=true, sent=false — satisfying the invariant. opted_out is never reset to false (no transition assigns it false). While opted_out stays true: send_attempt fires t_send_blocked (sent := false), t_send_allowed is disabled, and tick sets sent := false. No-op events leave state unchanged. Thus there is no reachable state with opted_out=true and sent=true. The property holds for the entire input space; the 7-event bound is not binding because the invariant is inductive (holds at the initial state opted_out=false and is preserved by every transition).
